subject: Ecology
title: Rewilding Cities
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Rewilding Cities.

hook.text: Rewilding a city means giving nature permission to be messy—unmowed, unsprayed, untamed.

buildup.text: Leaving patches of wild growth supports insects, wildflowers, and ground-nesting birds.

discovery.text: Berlin's abandoned Tempelhof Airport became one of Europe's largest urban wild spaces.

twist.text: Urban rewilding often clashes with residents who see wild areas as neglected, not natural.

climax.text: Education and signage can shift perception—'this is not neglect, this is habitat.'

punchline.text: Sometimes the best thing a city can do is nothing.

quiz.question: What is urban rewilding?
options: Allowing natural processes to reclaim urban spaces
